summary: Fix reader so cl:nil (single-colon external lookup) resolves to NIL instead of erroring, by disambiguating 'found NIL' from 'not found' via find-symbol status.

items.title: Reader: resolve cl:nil via status-aware external lookup + regression tests
items.description: In src/core/reader.c, the single-colon package-qualified branch (currently around lines 604-611) calls cl_package_find_external() and treats a CL_NIL return as 'not exported', then errors. This is wrong for the symbol NIL: cl_package_find_external() returns CL_NIL for BOTH 'symbol not found' AND 'found the symbol NIL' (NIL is interned and external in COMMON-LISP). Replace that lookup with cl_find_symbol_with_status(sym_name, sym_len, package, &status) and accept the symbol only when status == 2 (:EXTERNAL); otherwise emit the existing 'Symbol %s not exported from %s' error (preserving the read_suppress early-return). Do NOT accept status 3 (:INHERITED) — CLHS requires package:symbol to denote a symbol EXTERNAL in that package itself, matching current single-colon semantics. Note cl_find_symbol_with_status already normalizes the home NIL symbol to CL_NIL and is declared in package.h (already included by reader.c at line 4), so reading cl:nil will correctly yield NIL just as cl:t yields T. Leave the double-colon (cl::nil) branch (cl_intern_in) unchanged. Add a host regression test in tests/test_package.c next to eval_read_cl_qualified (~line 533): a TEST that asserts eval_print("(eq 'CL:NIL nil)") == "T" and eval_print("(null 'cl:nil)") == "T", and that a genuinely non-exported symbol still errors, e.g. eval_print of a CL-internal-only symbol like "CL:THIS-SYMBOL-DOES-NOT-EXIST" starts with "ERROR:". Register the new TEST with RUN(...) in the run list near lines 1080-1082. Also add an Amiga regression test in tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp next to the existing 'cl:sym' check (~line 1382), e.g. (check "cl:nil" t (eq 'CL:NIL nil)). Run `make test` to confirm host suite stays green.
